Description: Type safety for props and state

Do: Zustand for simple Redux for complex

Code Good: getItemLayout={(_, index) => ({ length: 50, offset: 50 * index, index })}

Description: Modern performant image component for React Native
Do: Use expo-image for caching, blurring, and performance
Don't: Use default Image for heavy lists or unmaintained libraries

Do: width and height for remote images
hitSlop for icons and small buttons
Test on iOS and Android devices

Description: Throttle WebSocket renders to prevent excessive re-rendering of dashboard widgets
Do: Throttle renders to 250ms min; use useRef for latest value and render on requestAnimationFrame or setInterval
Don't: Re-render entire screen per WebSocket message; update state on every tick
Code Good: const latestRef = useRef(value); useEffect(() => { const id = setInterval(() => setValue(latestRef.current), 250); return () => clearInterval(id); }, [])
Code Bad: onMessage: (data) => setState(data) // re-renders entire tree every message

Description: Smoothly interpolate dashboard numbers between old and new values using Reanimated
Do: Use Reanimated withTiming(newVal 300ms) or withSpring for natural feel; format with Intl.NumberFormat
Don't: Snap numbers instantly without transition (jarring for financial data)

Description: Use FlashList instead of FlatList for large frequently-updating dashboard lists
Do: Use @shopify/flash-list for 50+ items; set estimatedItemSize; use keyExtractor
Don't: Use FlatList for large frequently-updating lists without estimatedItemSize

Description: Use bottom sheet with snap points for card-to-detail drill-down pattern
Do: Use @gorhom/bottom-sheet with snapPoints for card-to-detail; animate from tap source
Don't: Navigate to new screen for every detail view (breaks dashboard context flow)

Description: Use native-thread gesture handler for swipeable dashboard card actions
Do: Use react-native-gesture-handler Swipeable for delete/archive actions on cards
Don't: Custom PanResponder for swipe (less performant and not on native thread)

Description: Use Skia for high-performance custom gauge and progress ring rendering
Do: Use @shopify/react-native-skia Path arc for semi-circle gauges and custom progress rings
Don't: Use SVG-based libraries for complex custom visualizations (slower rendering on RN)

Description: Use safe area insets for sticky dashboard headers to handle notch and Dynamic Island
Do: Use react-native-safe-area-context useSafeAreaInsets() for sticky dashboard headers
Don't: Hardcode status bar height (varies by device and Dynamic Island)

Description: Support landscape orientation for chart-focused views in dashboards
Do: Support landscape for chart views; use useWindowDimensions to adapt chart width/height
Don't: Lock to portrait only for chart-heavy dashboard screens
Code Good: const {width, height} = useWindowDimensions(); chartWidth = Math.max(width, height)

Description: Use consistent decimal formatting for financial numbers across the dashboard
Do: Use Intl.NumberFormat consistently; 2 decimals for fiat / 8 for crypto / 0 for large integers
Don't: Mix decimal precisions across the same screen

Description: Use green/red color with directional icons for profit/loss accessibility
Do: Green (#22C55E) for profit Red (#EF4444) for loss; ALWAYS pair with arrow-up/arrow-down icon
Don't: Rely on red/green color alone (8% of males are red-green colorblind)
Code Bad: <Text color='green'>+2.5%</Text> // no icon colorblind users miss it

Description: Use haptic feedback sparingly for important dashboard confirmations
Do: Use expo-haptics for trade confirmations / alert triggers / pull-to-refresh completion
Don't: Haptic feedback on every data update or tick (overuse numbs the user to feedback)

Description: Show stale data indicators when dashboard data is outdated or connection lost
Do: Show 'Last updated: X ago' label + muted overlay when stale; auto-retry on reconnect
Don't: Silently display stale data as if it is live and current
Code Good: <Text color='$muted'>Last updated {timeAgo}</Text> + opacity={0.6} on stale widgets
Code Bad: // no indication that data is 5 minutes old

Description: Wrap each dashboard widget in its own ErrorBoundary with retry
Do: Wrap each widget in ErrorBoundary; show retry button per widget; other widgets keep working
Don't: Let one failed API call or render error crash the entire dashboard screen
Code Bad: // no error boundary; one crash kills entire dashboard

Description: Support deep links to specific dashboard tabs and detail views
Do: Support deep links like app://dashboard/positions or app://dashboard/chart/BTCUSDT
Don't: Only deep link to top-level screens (makes sharing and notifications useless)
Code Bad: // only links to /dashboard no section routing

Description: Show skeleton placeholders matching each widget shape while loading
Do: Show skeleton matching widget dimensions; use moti/skeleton or placeholder lib; load widgets independently
Don't: Show a single full-screen spinner for the entire dashboard

Description: Pass app theme tokens into chart configuration for consistent dark/light mode
Do: Pass theme tokens to chartConfig colors (backgroundColor / labelColor derived from active theme)
Don't: Hardcode chart colors separately from app theme (breaks dark mode and consistency)
Code Bad: chartConfig={{ backgroundColor: '#fff', color: () => '#333' }} // breaks dark mode

Description: Provide visual feedback during and after pull-to-refresh on dashboards
Do: Use RefreshControl with tintColor matching theme accent; show last-refresh timestamp after pull
Don't: Silent refresh with no visual indication of what happened or when data was last refreshed
Code Bad: onRefresh={() => fetchData()} // no loading state shown

Description: Optimize tab content rendering for multi-tab dashboard layouts
Do: Use React.memo + useMemo for tab content components; lazy-render off-screen tabs
Don't: Re-render all tab contents on every segment switch (wasted renders)
